results.name: grok-4.3

blocking_gaps_for_10_10.gap: Claimed 802/802 tests in project facts but trust bar and demo explicitly show 623/623 engine tests; also no visible regression harness output or NTI cohort test logs in demo.
blocking_gaps_for_10_10.smallest_fix: Update all references (trust bar, README, demo) to a single verified number and embed a one-click 'run tests' button that prints the 802/802 summary.

blocking_gaps_for_10_10.gap: Mermaid diagrams and invariants.md are referenced but flows.json/arch_mind.json/bitnet_pairs.json are external fetches; no inline README excerpt or full architecture.md content visible.
blocking_gaps_for_10_10.smallest_fix: Embed the full architecture.md and clinicalmem_invariants.md as collapsible sections in the demo or add a 'View full docs' modal with the Mermaid source.

blocking_gaps_for_10_10.gap: Safety check, what-if simulator, BitNet classify, and federation mock are all client-side simulations with no live backend call or real engine execution shown.
blocking_gaps_for_10_10.smallest_fix: Add a visible 'Connect to live engine' toggle that hits a real /api endpoint (even if mocked server-side) and shows actual latency + hash computation.

strengths: Hero animation with rotating audit-chain rings, staggered layer dots, and landing 'CONTRAINDICATED' stamp is genuinely impressive and healthcare-appropriate in 5 seconds.
Deep clinical standards integration (FHIR R4 with NPI-validated practitioners, RxNorm/SNOMED/UMLS, 21 typed federation invariants) backed by verified MD co-author.
Reproducibility primitives (Merkle chain, Ed25519, plan_hash replay, BitNet Q16.16) are wired end-to-end and demoed with in-browser SHA-256 verification.
parsed.delta_from_round_3: R3 UI score collapsed to 6.00 because the GitHub repo went private and no demo.html was visible. R4 supplies the full 2125-line live demo source, enabling direct scoring of typography (Lexend + Fraunces + JetBrains Mono), custom trust/premium/po palettes, animated rings, interactive what-if + BitNet replay, and Mermaid flows. This single change lifts ui_visual_design from 6 to 9 and demo_quality_judge_wow from low to 9. Additional deltas: ASCII diagrams replaced by Mermaid, federation contract now explicitly shows X25519 + ChaCha20-Poly1305 wrapping before MIC@2 serialization, and arch-mind L1 9/9 + Phase B invariants documented. Composite rises from 8.72 to 8.7 with the UI recovery outweighing minor test-number inconsistency.

blocking_gaps_for_10_10.gap: No evidence of hardware-accelerated BitNet inference (e.g., NPU/TPU kernels) or quantization-aware training for the ternary weights. The 19 KB claim is impressive but lacks runtime benchmarks across ARM/x86.
blocking_gaps_for_10_10.smallest_fix: Add a single-page supplement showing latency/throughput on Pi Zero, iPhone A16, and Intel AVX2 with/without quantization. Include a link to a HuggingFace Space with live inference.

blocking_gaps_for_10_10.gap: While federation invariants are novel, the demo doesn't show a live multi-site deployment or compare against federated RAG baselines (e.g., FedAvg + differential privacy).
blocking_gaps_for_10_10.smallest_fix: Add a 30-second video of two mock hospitals exchanging findings with PHI gates in action, or a side-by-side table comparing ClinicalMem's typed invariants vs. generic federated learning.

blocking_gaps_for_10_10.gap: 802/802 tests passing is excellent, but no breakdown of test types (unit/integration/fuzz) or mutation testing results for the BitNet layer.
blocking_gaps_for_10_10.smallest_fix: Add a badge in the README showing mutation score (e.g., '92% mutation coverage on Layer 4.5') with a link to the mutation report.

strengths: The BitNet b1.58 Layer 4.5 is a masterstroke—19 KB weights with 85.7% precision on contraindicated pairs is a reproducibility primitive that regulators will love. The in-browser SHA-256 replay demo is a mic-drop moment.

Clinical credibility is airtight: NPI-validated MD co-author, 21 CFR Part 11 audit chain, and Synthea cohort with NIH-recommended synthetic data. The penicillin/amoxicillin cross-reactivity demo is a real-world killer use case.

UI/UX is best-in-class for healthcare AI. The audit-chain rings animation, Lexend typography, and trust/premium palette choices show deep domain expertise. The 'Submitted to Agents Assemble' stamp is a brilliant hackathon touch.
Federation design is patent-pending and actually solves PHI leakage with typed runtime invariants—not just policy docs. The X25519 + ChaCha20-Poly1305 contract is production-grade.
Documentation polish is elite: Mermaid diagrams render consistently across GitHub/PDF/demo, and the 6 healthcare-specific invariants are documented before they're even enforced in CI.
parsed.delta_from_round_3: UI score rebounded from 6.0 to 10.0 after the Cloudflare Pages demo became accessible. The Mermaid diagrams in architecture.md and the in-browser BitNet replay demo added 1.5 points to the composite. The only regression is the GitHub repo remaining private (costing 0.5 points in open_source_ecosystem_fit), but the demo's self-contained nature mitigates this.

parsed.delta_from_round_3: R3 UI score catastrophically dropped to 6.00 because the GitHub repo went private and we couldn't see the demo visually. R4 fix: the full demo.html (2125 lines, 134 KB) was inlined verbatim. The visual design is sophisticated — editorial typography (Lexend for NIH accessibility, Fraunces for NEJM editorial accent), custom trust-teal clinical palette, three rotating audit-chain rings in the hero, and a stamp-landing animation for the CONTRAINDICATED badge. The UI score recovers from 6.00 → 9. The demo makes a judge say 'wow' in 5 seconds because the hero animation is genuinely impressive and the patient card feels like a real EHR. Composite climbs from 8.72 → 9.10. The gap to 10/10 is that the What-If simulator and BitNet classifier are lookup-based mocks rather than live inference.

results.name: nvidia-deepseek-v3.2

blocking_gaps_for_10_10.gap: Single MD advisor, no hospital affiliation or institutional IRB validation.
blocking_gaps_for_10_10.smallest_fix: Add a second verified MD from a major academic medical center (e.g., Mayo, Mass General) and secure a letter of collaboration from a hospital's innovation lab.

blocking_gaps_for_10_10.gap: Synthetic cohort (Synthea) is IRB-exempt but not a real-world validation. No evidence of external audit (e.g., by a QSR consultant).
blocking_gaps_for_10_10.smallest_fix: Run a pilot with a de-identified real dataset from a research partner (e.g., MIMIC-IV) and publish a third-party audit report.

blocking_gaps_for_10_10.gap: 802/802 tests passing is strong, but no mutation testing or fuzzing evidence for security-critical PHI gate.
blocking_gaps_for_10_10.smallest_fix: Add a mutation testing suite (e.g., mutmut) for the PHI classifier and publish coverage of adversarial examples.

strengths: Demo is a masterpiece of clinical-AI storytelling: animated audit rings, live patient card, interactive what-if simulator, and in-browser SHA-256 replay create instant judge 'wow'.
Apache-2.0 + patent grant is gold standard for healthcare open source; MCP/A2A integration shows real ecosystem fit.
Technical depth is exceptional: BitNet b1.58 ternary weights, 21 typed federation invariants, MIC@2 transport, and Merkle audit chain are not toy features.
parsed.delta_from_round_3: R4 fixed the catastrophic UI drop (6.00 in R3) by inlining the live demo source. The demo.html is a 134KB production-grade dashboard with Tailwind, custom clinical typography (Lexend, Source Sans 3), animated hero (rotating audit rings, layer-pulse dots), interactive patient simulator, and Mermaid diagrams. This proves the UI/visual design is not just a placeholder but a polished, accessible clinical interface. Scores for ui_visual_design and demo_quality_judge_wow jump to 10/10, pulling the composite mean from 8.72 to 9.30.
